recommended_action: 1. Check connector tightness at RRU and antenna. 2. Inspect for water damage using moisture indicators. 3. Perform line sweep test with site analyzer. 4. Check for cable kinks or damage. 5. Verify antenna grounding.
1. Check cooling fans are operational. 2. Verify ambient temperature at site. 3. Clean dust filters. 4. Check for blocked ventilation. 5. If persists, consider load reduction.
1. Check fiber integrity. 2. Verify switch port utilization. 3. Check for packet drops on transport. 4. Consider traffic prioritization adjustment.
1. Check power supply status. 2. Verify baseband unit connectivity. 3. Check for hardware faults. 4. Review O&M logs for root cause. 5. Escalate if hardware failure suspected.
1. Verify remote electrical tilt (RET) motor status. 2. Check RET control cable. 3. Re-calibrate antenna tilt. 4. If motor faulty, schedule site visit.
1. Check UDM connectivity. 2. Verify AUSF authentication status. 3. Review AMF logs for rejection reasons. 4. Check subscriber profile consistency. 5. Verify HSS/UDR synchronization.
1. Check fiber/cable integrity on Xn link. 2. Verify router/switch interface status. 3. Check for CRC errors. 4. Review traffic patterns for congestion.
1. Check GPS antenna and cable. 2. Verify primary sync source availability. 3. Switch to backup sync source if available. 4. Check IEEE 1588 PTP configuration. 5. Escalate to transport team.
1. Review license utilization. 2. Contact vendor for renewal quote. 3. Plan license renewal or feature deactivation. 4. Update license budget.
1. Check mains power availability. 2. Verify rectifier operation. 3. Check battery health/age. 4. Plan battery replacement if degraded. 5. Consider generator deployment for extended outage.
